concept: Liberate / Set Free / Release From Bondage
description: To set free from constraint or oppression; the act of creating freedom. 'Renoa' names the freeing action—what transforms 'ralu' (free) from possibility to reality.
sound_symbolism: The word releases with purpose. 'Re' resonates with freeing action, bonds dissolving. 'No' settles with grounded change, new state forming. Then 'a' opens to liberated being.
grammatical_notes: Functions as verb: 'mi renoa' (I liberate). Creates the state of 'ralu' (free). Related to 'phura' as action to quality.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Liberation as practice—the ongoing work of freeing mind from what binds.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Liberating voice—creating conditions where silenced voices can speak.
